Justification: The kettle shall have a capacity of 1.5 liters, a reduction from the previous 1.7-liter suggestion, to minimize material usage and energy consumption per boil, aligning more closely with sustainability goals while still meeting the needs of most households.; A comparative analysis indicates that a 1.5-liter capacity can still serve 4-6 standard cups, which is sufficient for typical family use, and offers a quantifiable reduction in material footprint and energy demand compared to larger capacities.; The functional maximum fill line shall be clearly marked, with the 1.5-liter capacity referring to this usable volume, ensuring safety and efficient operation.; Dimensions and weight shall be optimized for ease of handling and storage, with target maximum dimensions of 250mm (height) x 150mm (width) x 220mm (depth) and a maximum dry weight of 1.2 kg.

The kettle shall boil 1.5 liters of water from ambient temperature (20°C) to boiling point (100°C) within 4.5 minutes.; This revised target of 4.5 minutes for a 1.5L capacity maintains a competitive boiling speed while acknowledging the reduced volume. The energy efficiency is optimized by balancing power and time, avoiding excessive wattage that might lead to less efficient heating cycles.; Boiling time for minimum fill (0.5 liters) shall not exceed 2 minutes.; The ambient temperature assumption of 20°C will be used for testing, with acknowledgement that performance may vary in different climatic conditions.

The kettle shall have a power rating between 1800W and 2000W, targeting the lower end of the previous range to enhance overall energy efficiency.; This power range is selected to provide rapid boiling for the 1.5-liter capacity while remaining compatible with standard household electrical circuits (e.g., 10-13A circuits in the UK/EU, 15A in North America).; The target efficiency at the upper end of this range (2000W) shall be a minimum of 85% energy transfer to water. Efficiency at the lower end (1800W) should be maximized through superior insulation and heating element design.; A lower wattage option (e.g., 1500W) with a slightly longer boiling time (approx. 5.5 minutes for 1.5L) will be explored as a potential variant for markets prioritizing extreme energy savings over speed.

The exterior casing shall be constructed from a minimum of 75% post-consumer recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade) or a certified compostable bioplastic (e.g., PLA-based composite) suitable for industrial composting.; For recycled stainless steel, sourcing shall adhere to recognized standards such as the Global Recycled Standard (GRS). For bioplastics, durability requirements include scratch resistance (Mohs 3), UV stability (no significant color change after 100 hours of exposure), and impact resistance (withstanding a 0.5m drop test without cracking).; The trade-off between durability and biodegradability will be managed by prioritizing material longevity for the primary use phase. The chosen bioplastic must meet rigorous testing for microplastic release during its intended degradation pathway.; Sustainably sourced recycled stainless steel will be verified through supplier declarations and certifications.

The interior of the kettle and the heating element shall be made of food-grade 304 stainless steel, with a minimum thickness of 0.6mm to ensure durability and prevent deformation under thermal stress.; Efforts will be made to incorporate at least 30% recycled content into the stainless steel used for the interior and heating element, subject to meeting all safety and performance requirements.; While stainless steel recycling is energy-intensive, its inherent durability and recyclability make it the preferred choice over materials with lower embodied energy but poorer end-of-life prospects or safety concerns.

The kettle shall consume less than 0.2W in standby mode, exceeding the previous 0.5W target to significantly reduce phantom load and enhance sustainability.; This aggressive target is achievable with modern low-power electronics and will be validated through rigorous testing.

The kettle shall feature an automatic shut-off mechanism that activates within 2°C of the boiling point (98°C-100°C) or immediately when the kettle is lifted from its base.; The auto shut-off sensor and mechanism shall be designed and tested to endure a minimum of 5,000 cycles, ensuring reliability throughout the product's expected lifespan.

The kettle shall incorporate boil-dry protection that deactivates the heating element within 5 seconds of detecting no water.; Upon activation, the boil-dry protection will trigger a distinct audible beep and a visual alert via the indicator light, informing the user of the condition. The kettle will require manual reset (e.g., unplugging and replugging) after cooling down to resume operation.

The kettle shall be designed to withstand a minimum of 3,000 full boiling cycles (1.5 liters from 20°C to 100°C), with key components like the thermostat, heating element, and switch rated for a significantly higher cycle count.; This lifespan will be supported by the use of high-quality components, including a durable bimetallic thermostat and robust electrical connections, and will be validated through accelerated life testing.; A standard warranty of 2 years will be offered, aligning with the expected durability.

The kettle, when empty and at ambient temperature, shall withstand a drop from a height of 0.5 meters onto a tiled surface without functional failure or cracking of the main casing.; Functional failure is defined as the inability to boil water or operate safety features. Minor cosmetic scuffs are acceptable.; For a full kettle, a drop height of 0.3 meters will be the maximum considered for testing, due to the increased risk of injury and severe damage. The base and handle will be specifically reinforced to withstand such impacts.

The handle shall be designed to maintain a maximum surface temperature of 45°C during operation and require a maximum grip force of 20N for a secure hold, accommodating a wide range of users.; The handle shape will be contoured to fit the natural curve of the hand, with a non-slip texture, and tested to ensure stability during a simulated pouring test with a full kettle at a 45° tilt.; The handle-to-body connection will be robust, designed to withstand repeated stress.

The spout shall be designed for drip-free pouring, with a maximum allowable drip volume of 0.5ml within 10 seconds after pouring ceases.; The spout opening shall be optimized for a flow rate of approximately 100-150 ml/second at a 45° pouring angle, minimizing splashing.; The spout interior shall be smooth and easily accessible for cleaning to prevent mineral buildup.

The lid shall open with a single button press requiring no more than 5N of force, or a smooth manual action requiring no more than 10N of force.; The lid shall open to a minimum angle of 75° to facilitate easy filling and cleaning.; The lid shall create a secure seal during boiling, preventing steam leakage exceeding 5% of total steam volume, and withstand internal pressure generated at 100°C.

The kettle shall feature a minimum opening diameter of 80mm for easy access to the interior for cleaning and descaling.; The interior surface shall be smooth stainless steel, designed to resist limescale buildup. Descaling is expected to be required only once every 3 months with typical hard water conditions, and easily performed with standard descaling solutions.; The removable filter shall be dishwasher safe (top rack), and the main body interior shall be easily cleaned with a soft cloth and descaling agent.

A clear, high-contrast water level indicator shall be integrated, with markings for 0.5L (minimum) and 1.5L (maximum fill), accurate to within ± 5% of the indicated volume.; The indicator window shall be made of durable, scratch-resistant borosilicate glass or high-quality transparent polymer, readable under various lighting conditions, and positioned for easy viewing from a standing user's perspective.; Markings shall be laser-etched or otherwise permanently affixed to resist fading or wear.

The power base shall incorporate a robust cord storage system, with at least three pegs or a simple reel mechanism, allowing the 0.75-meter power cord to be neatly wound and secured.; The cord storage mechanism shall be durable and easy to use, contributing to a tidy countertop and preventing cord damage.

The kettle shall operate at a noise level not exceeding 58 dB during the final stages of boiling (when water is actively bubbling), measured at a distance of 1 meter.; This target of 58 dB, a reduction from 60 dB, aims to create a quieter user experience. Noise reduction will be achieved through optimized heating element design, internal baffling, and vibration dampening materials where appropriate.; The measurement will be conducted with a full 1.5L kettle from 20°C to 100°C.

The kettle shall feature a modern, minimalist design with clean lines, a matte finish, and a neutral color palette (e.g., brushed stainless steel, matte white, charcoal grey).; Visual references and mood boards will be developed to clearly define the desired aesthetic, emphasizing a premium yet understated look.; Textures will be considered to enhance tactile appeal and perceived quality. The form factor will be compact and visually unobtrusive.

The packaging shall be constructed from 100% post-consumer recycled cardboard, with soy-based inks for printing. Plastic use shall be limited to essential protective elements, which must be made from recycled and recyclable materials.; Packaging weight shall not exceed 15% of the product's net weight, and overall volume will be minimized through efficient design.; All packaging materials will be clearly marked for recycling.

The kettle shall be designed for disassembly using standard tools (e.g., Phillips head screwdriver). Components will be joined with screws or snap-fits where feasible, avoiding permanent adhesives.; All major plastic and metal components will be clearly labeled according to ISO 11469 standards to facilitate efficient recycling.; A program for providing key spare parts (e.g., heating element, lid mechanism) for at least 5 years post-production will be established to promote repairability and extend product life. Partnerships with certified e-waste recyclers will be explored for responsible end-of-life collection and processing.

The kettle shall achieve CE marking, RoHS compliance, and Energy Star certification (or equivalent regional energy efficiency standard).; For Energy Star, the target shall be to meet or exceed the current energy efficiency criteria for electric kettles.; Relevant regional safety certifications such as UL (North America) or equivalent will be obtained based on the primary target markets. Consideration will be given to certifications related to sustainable manufacturing (e.g., ISO 14001 for the manufacturing facility) and ethical sourcing.

Variable temperature control shall be implemented, offering settings for 70°C, 80°C, 90°C, and 100°C, with an accuracy of ± 2°C.; A 'keep warm' function will be included, consuming no more than 10W to maintain the selected temperature for up to 30 minutes.; The user interface for temperature selection will be intuitive, utilizing simple buttons and clear LED indicators, ensuring ease of use without adding unnecessary complexity.

A single, subtle LED indicator light (e.g., cool white or soft blue) shall be used to indicate power status and heating. Its brightness will be optimized to be visible in daylight but not distracting in low light.; The indicator light will also serve as a secondary alert for boil-dry protection activation, flashing in conjunction with an audible tone.; The power consumption of the indicator light shall not exceed 0.1W.

The power base shall have a non-slip surface (e.g., rubberized feet) and be designed to withstand a tilt angle of at least 15° without the kettle tipping over when fully loaded.; The electrical connection between the kettle and the base shall be secure and robust, designed to withstand repeated docking and undocking without wear or loss of connection.; The weight distribution of the kettle and base will be optimized to ensure a low center of gravity and inherent stability.
